metadata.working_title: Deciphering molecular heterogeneity and dynamics of human hippocampal neural stem cells across neonatal, adult, aging, and stroke-induced injury states

abstract_sketch.problem: Whether adult human hippocampal neurogenesis persists and how hippocampal neural stem cells (NSCs) behave across life stages and after injury remain debated, because human tissue is scarce and markers used in rodents lack specificity in humans.
abstract_sketch.gap: Although accumulated studies support the existence of human adult hippocampal neurogenesis, the molecular heterogeneity, developmental potentials, and quiescence-activation dynamics of human hippocampal NSCs across neonatal, adult, aging, and injury contexts remain unclear.
abstract_sketch.approach: We generated a single-nucleus RNA-seq (snRNA-seq) atlas of 10 post-mortem human hippocampi (neonatal D4 n=1, adult 31-32y n=2, aging 50-68y n=6, stroke-injured 48y n=1), yielding 92,966 high-quality nuclei across 16 major populations; we combined cross-species comparison, single-cell Hierarchical Poisson Factorization (scHPF), Seurat FindAllMarkers, RNA-velocity, and Monocle pseudotime analyses with validating immunostaining.
abstract_sketch.key_results: We resolved qNSC1, qNSC2, pNSC, aNSC, and neuroblast populations; identified novel human-specific neurogenic-lineage markers (LRRC3B, RHOJ, SLC4A4, GLI3 for qNSCs; CHI3L1, EGFR for pNSCs; CALM3, NEUROD2, NRGN, NRN1 for neuroblasts) that avoid GABAergic-interneuron contamination of DCX/PROX1/STMN2; showed that most NSCs enter deep quiescence during aging; and demonstrated that stroke injury reactivates quiescent NSCs, with pNSCs and aNSCs recovering to 17.3% (322/1861) and 11.7% (218/1861) of the neurogenic lineage in the injured hippocampus.
abstract_sketch.impact: The atlas and marker set provide a foundational human-hippocampal NSC reference, reconcile conflicting reports of adult neurogenesis, and suggest that reserved quiescent NSCs can be reawakened by pathological signals.

sections.title: Introduction
content_bullets: Continuous learning and memory depend on developmental and adult neurogenesis in the dentate gyrus (DG), whose neural stem cells sustain granule-cell production.
Rodent adult hippocampal neurogenesis is well characterized, but whether it persists in humans has been debated, with immunostaining studies reaching opposite conclusions.
snRNA-seq bypasses fixation and marker-specificity biases, and three recent studies have produced conflicting human hippocampal snRNA-seq datasets.
Mouse NSCs transition between quiescent and active states and are reactivated by ischemic injury; whether similar dynamics operate in humans is unknown.
We profile 10 donor hippocampi spanning neonatal, adult, aging, and stroke-injured states to dissect NSC heterogeneity, dynamics, and reactivation, and to identify human-specific lineage markers.

subsections.title: Single-nucleus atlas of the human hippocampus across ages and injury
content_bullets: 10 post-mortem donors (D4 n=1; 31y, 32y n=2; 50-68y n=6; stroke 48y n=1) processed for 10x Genomics snRNA-seq on anterior-mid DG.
Sequenced 99,635 nuclei; 92,966 retained after removing debris, aggregates, and cells with >20% mitochondrial transcripts; median 3001 genes/nucleus.
Cell composition varies substantially across life stages: pNSC and aNSC counts fall with aging and partially recover after stroke; granule cells are depleted in the injured hippocampus.

subsections.title: Heterogeneity and molecular features of human hippocampal NSCs
content_bullets: Cross-species integration with mouse, pig, and rhesus-macaque datasets (Hochgerner 2018; Franjic 2022) places human AS2/qNSC and pNSC with radial glia-like cells, and human aNSC/NB with nIPC/NB of other species.

AS2/qNSC subclusters into astrocyte2, qNSC1, and qNSC2; qNSC1 preferentially expresses HOPX and is deeply quiescent, qNSC2 expresses LPAR1 plus neurogenic genes (STMN1, PROX1, SIRT2, ST18) showing initial lineage potential.

pNSCs highly express HOPX, VIM, SOX2, SOX4, CCND2 and are enriched for Wnt, Notch, Hippo, and neurogenesis GO terms; aNSCs additionally express SOX11 and are enriched for neuron differentiation, axonogenesis, and synapse organization GO terms.
Cell-cycle scoring places qNSC1/2 in G0/G1 and aNSCs predominantly in S/G2/M, confirming distinct quiescent and active states.

subsections.title: Novel markers distinguishing NSC subtypes and neuroblasts
content_bullets: Combining scHPF and Seurat FindAllMarkers identified qNSC markers LRRC3B, RHOJ, SLC4A4, GLI3; pNSC markers CHI3L1 and EGFR; and NB candidates NRGN, NRN1, HECW1.

Classical NB/immature-GC markers (DCX, PROX1, CALB2, STMN2, NSG2, SNAP25, BASP1, HECW1) are also broadly expressed in human GABAergic interneurons (validated by DLX1, GABRG3, GAD1/2, RELN, VIP and cross-species transcriptome similarity > 0.95).
Co-immunostaining confirmed SST+ interneurons are DCX+ in primate hippocampus, establishing an interneuron-contamination risk for canonical NB markers.

Filtering scHPF/FindMarker top NB genes against the interneuron transcriptome yielded NB-specific markers CALM3, TTC9B, NRGN, FXYD7, NRN1, GNG3, TCEAL5, TMSB10, NEUROD2, validated in adult and aging samples.

subsections.title: Developmental trajectory and molecular cascades in neonatal hippocampus
content_bullets: RNA-velocity in D4 hippocampus shows pNSCs bifurcate toward quiescence/AS2 or toward aNSCs; Monocle pseudotime rooted at pNSC yields pNSC -> aNSC -> NB -> two granule-cell neurons N1 and N2.
HOPX/SOX2/VIM peak at the root and decline with differentiation; DCX/STMN2 rise along the lineage; SYT1 peaks at the terminus.
Immunostaining of D4 DG confirms HOPX+NES+ radial glia, Ki67+NES+ proliferating NSCs, and PSA-NCAM+ neuroblasts clustered in the GCL.

Four pseudotime gene clusters capture proliferation/Wnt at the root (VIM, GFAP, SOX6, CD44, CHI3L1, TNC, EGFR, HOPX), mid-trajectory neurogenesis (NRGN, STMN2, NEUROD6), axon guidance/synaptic function at the N1 terminus (SV2B, SYT6, SYN2) and dendritic-spine/excitatory-postsynaptic at the N2 terminus (MYT1, GIRK2); trajectory-associated TFs include PBX3, PROX1, GLIS3, RFX4, TEAD1 (early) and POU6F2, FOXP2, THRB, ETV1, NR4A3, BCL11A, NCALD, LUZP2, RARB (late).

subsections.title: Most NSCs enter deep quiescence during aging
content_bullets: qNSC1/qNSC2 ratios rise with age while pNSC and aNSC populations decline sharply from neonatal to adult to aging; neuroblasts persist into adulthood but are markedly reduced in aging.

Along aging, lineage-wide up-regulation of astrocyte/quiescence genes (LRRC3B, RHOJ, SLC4A4) and down-regulation of pNSC (HOPX, SOX2, VIM, NES, CHI3L1), aNSC (ASCL1, EOMES, MKI67) and NB (STMN2, DCX) markers.
Immunostaining of D4, 32y, 50y, and 56y DG confirms loss of HOPX/VIM/NES/CHI3L1 with age and retention of PSA-NCAM only through adult stages.

qNSC1/2 aging DEGs up-regulate growth-inhibition and differentiation genes (BRINP1, CABLES1, TENM2, CNTN1, RANBP3L, NDRG2, MBP, PLP1, MOBP) and down-regulate stem/regeneration genes (LPAR1, TNC, CASC15, SOX2, SOX4, HOPX, VIM); GOs point to negative growth regulation, NSC-pool maintenance, and glial differentiation.

subsections.title: Stroke injury reactivates quiescent NSCs in the adult hippocampus
content_bullets: Stroke-injured (48y) hippocampus shows elevated apoptosis/DNA-damage/autophagy gene expression in GCs and GABA-INs and TUNEL+ nuclei absent in other adult/aging samples, confirming injury.

pNSCs and aNSCs re-emerge in the injured hippocampus while qNSCs decline, suggesting injury-induced reactivation; NES+KI67+, NES+VIM+ and NES+CHI3L1+ radial-glia-morphology active NSCs detected by immunostaining.

After separating reactive astrocytes from reactivated NSCs via gene-set scoring, pNSC and aNSC fractions of the neurogenic lineage reach 17.3% (322/1861) and 11.7% (218/1861), comparable to neonatal ratios; qNSC1 falls to 23.4% (413/1258) and qNSC2 to 11.1% (214/1258) in injury vs 32.8%/17.0% in adult and 57.8% (310/536)/22.6% (121/536) in aged.

Monocle pseudotime of the injury-sample neurogenic lineage originates from qNSC1/2 -> pNSC -> aNSC/NB with HOPX/PAX6 at the root, VIM/CD44/TNC/CHI3L1 at the middle, and SOX2/STMN2 at the terminus; four gene clusters capture Wnt/regeneration at the root, neurogenesis in the middle, and neuronal (MAP2, SYT1, SYN2) plus glial (PLP1, MBP, MOG) fates at the two endpoints.

Stroke-up-regulated lineage genes span regeneration (TNC, GPC6, CRYAB, GBP2), autophagy/proliferation (VMP1, CHI3L1, SPP1, VIM, ITGB1, CYR61, CD63, ACTN4, ELL2, SPOCD1), inflammation/metabolism (IFI44L, RASGEF1B, STAT1), and metabolism (ACTN2, GBE1, NAMPT).

sections.title: Discussion
content_bullets: This atlas reconciles conflicting reports of human adult hippocampal neurogenesis by showing that qNSC, pNSC and aNSC are rare in healthy aging but can be reactivated by injury.
Human-specific NB markers (CALM3, NEUROD2, NRGN, NRN1) and qNSC markers (LRRC3B, RHOJ, SLC4A4) overcome the GABAergic-interneuron contamination that confounds DCX/PROX1/STMN2-based identification.

Integration of Zhou (2022), Wang (2022), Franjic (2022), and Ayhan (2021) datasets suggests that reports of neurogenesis in aged donors may reflect disease- or injury-induced activation rather than baseline turnover.

Developmental trajectory and TF cascade establish a reference for human hippocampal neurogenesis; the short post-stroke survival window (2 days) likely limits full neuroblast maturation, consistent with the longer human neuroblast maturation period reported previously.

Limitations: neonatal and stroke groups have n=1; snRNA-seq cannot fully separate qNSCs from astrocytes; protein-level and lineage-tracing validation remain necessary. Future integrative epigenomic/proteomic/metabolomic profiling and non-invasive lineage tracing are needed.

sections.title: Materials and Methods
content_bullets: Human donors: 10 post-mortem hippocampi (1F, 9M); neonatal D4 n=1; adult 31y/32y n=2; aging 50y, 56y, 60y, 64y-1, 64y-2, 68y; stroke-injured 48y; anterior-mid hippocampus with DG used for snRNA-seq and immunostaining.

Nuclei isolation: chilled lysis buffer (10 mM Tris-HCl, 10 mM NaCl, 3 mM MgCl2, 0.1% Nonidet P40), 15 min on ice with gentle shaking, 30 um MACS SmartStrainer, 500xg/5 min/4C centrifugation, resuspension in 1X PBS/1.0% BSA/0.2 U/ul RNase inhibitor.
Cynomolgus monkey control: female, 3 months, 2.3 kg body weight.
Filtering: removed debris, aggregates, cells with >20% mitochondrial transcript fraction; Wilcoxon rank-sum for markers (avg log-expression difference > 0.5, p < 0.05).

Analysis: Seurat (FindAllMarkers), scHPF (Levitin 2019), Monocle 2 (estimateDispersions, reduceDimension DDRTree) with BEAM branch analysis (q < 1e-4), RNA velocity, Cell Cycle Scoring, AddModuleScore for gene sets, clusterProfiler for GO.

Immunostaining: 4% PFA fixation up to 24 h, 30% sucrose cryoprotection, 10 um sections on Leica CM1950, blocking with 2% Triton X-100 2 h, VECTOR TrueView autofluorescence quencher, 3% BSA, Alexa Fluor 488/555/647 secondaries, DAPI; TUNEL with 20 uL labeling mix at 37C for 120 min.
